verdicts.reason: death-of-dalton-greyjoy → fair-isle-rebellion: Text explicitly narrates fleet dissolution (line 93: 'began to dissolve, as captain after captain slipped away') immediately before the rebellion. ENABLES correctly preserves collective agency of smallfolk/knights who made the free choice to rise.
alyn-velaryon PARTICIPATES_IN storm: Alyn commanded the fleet that suffered the losses. Role edge is straightforward and grounded.

gunthor-slays-alester-wynch SUB_BEAT_OF fair-isle-rebellion: Text places this killing (line 99) as the resolution of Faircastle holdout within the wider rebellion, making it a beat inside the named event hub.

drazenkos-secret-at-sunspear MOTIVATES ransom-pact: Drazenko's whispered secret (line 119) drove Oakenfist's decision to sail for Lys. MOTIVATES correctly targets the decision-event, preserving Oakenfist's human agency in negotiating terms.

capture-of-prince-viserys MOTIVATES ransom-pact: 130 AC capture motivated the 134 AC ransom negotiation. The text (line 181) directly credits the capture as the underlying condition. MOTIVATES targets decision-event per agency preservation.

rhaena-first-flies-morning SUB_BEAT_OF lysene-spring: Text explicitly names Rhaena flying Morning as one of two 'momentous events' that brought joy during the Spring (line 85). SUB_BEAT_OF is correctly used.

poisoning-of-gaemon-palehair MOTIVATES aegon-iii-targaryen: Gaemon's death causes Aegon's gloom to deepen and return (line 121: 'his old gloom had settled over him once again'). Character-target MOTIVATES per vocabulary definition.

secret-siege MOTIVATES rowans-forced-confession: Text contextually narrates the causal chain — Marston refused assault (lines 25-37), relying on hunger/words. On day 12 (line 39), Rowan is brought forth and tortured to confess. Context makes clear the siege stalemate motivated the extraction attempt. MOTIVATES targets the decision-event.

rowans-forced-confession MOTIVATES aegon-iii-targaryen: Rowan's exposed false confession provokes the king's counterstroke—declaring Rowan innocent and ordering arrests (line 63). Character-target MOTIVATES reflects Aegon's decisive response to exposed torture.

rowans-forced-confession MOTIVATES death-of-marston-waters: Confession exposure → Graceford names conspirators → Marston attempts arrest of Flowers → Flowers stabs Waters (line 79). Chain of individual decisions justifies MOTIVATES; no agency collapse.
mervyn-flowers AGENT_IN death-of-marston-waters: Mervyn is the killer (line 79). Standard role edge, correctly grounded.
marston-waters VICTIM_IN death-of-marston-waters: Waters is the victim (line 79). Standard role edge.

rowans-forced-confession MOTIVATES aegon-iii-dismisses-the-regents-and-hand: Text explicitly links the conspiracy year trauma to Aegon's mistrustful disposition (line 209: 'the events of the past year had only served to deepen his suspicions'). This disposition directly shapes his dismissal of regents on his 16th nameday (lines 269-283). Causal link explicitly narrated.

aegon-iii-dismisses-the-regents-and-hand MOTIVATES torrhen-manderly: Manderly's dismissal transforms him from loyal servant to enemy (line 295: 'made an enemy of a man who had been amongst his most leal and devoted servants'). Text notes he 'would he ever forgive the king' (line 290). Character-target MOTIVATES.

aegon-iii-targaryen AGENT_IN aegon-iii-dismisses-the-regents-and-hand: Aegon enters the council chamber and curtly dismisses regents/Hand (line 269: 'You are sitting in my chair'). He is clearly the agent. Role edge.
torrhen-manderly PARTICIPATES_IN aegon-iii-dismisses-the-regents-and-hand: Manderly is the Hand being dismissed (line 283). He is present and directly addressed. Role edge.

death-of-thaddeus-rowan ENABLES gathering-of-the-lords: Rowan's death leaves regency vacant. Text (line 115) notes Munkun served as regent/Hand, then (line 119) lords were summoned to 136 AC gathering. Narrative chain is clear: vacancy → Munkun's solo rule → summons needed for new regents. ENABLES preserves lords' collective agency in answering summons.

gathering-of-the-lords ENABLES selection-of-three-regents-by-lot: Text (line 127) shows lords assembled, failed to agree, then Munkun proposed lot-selection. The assembly created the condition for the proposal. ENABLES is correct.

gathering-of-the-lords ENABLES torrhen-manderly-chosen-as-hand: Assembled lords debated and selected Manderly (line 129-130). Their collective deliberation and choice was the necessary precondition. ENABLES preserves their agency.

monstrous-dragonbirth-on-driftmark → quarrel-over-the-dragon-eggs: Change type TRIGGERS to CAUSES. The text shows: dragonbirth news (line 103) → troubles Aegon → Aegon commands ban on dragon eggs → Viserys becomes wroth (line 104-105). Aegon's decision to ban eggs is a deliberative act mediating the link. Per VERIFY-RULES, TRIGGERS requires 'nothing decisional between,' but Aegon's command is decisional. CAUSES allows mediation and better reflects the narrative.

capture-of-lysaro-rogare ENABLES scourging-of-lysaro-rogare: Capture was the precondition for trial and sentencing (line 185). The sentence explicitly details the scourging. ENABLES correctly identifies the enabling precondition.

daeron-i-targaryen AGENT_IN conquest-of-dorne: Line 41 states 'Daeron conquered Dorne.' Role edge is straightforward and well-grounded. Proposal correctly defers the fuller causal claim ('unable to hold → died young') for insufficient grounding in this unit.

new_node_checks.reason: Type event.assassination is accurate (Tess kills him in bed with his own dagger, line 85-87). Era targaryen-rule and year 133 AC are correct. Identity statement accurately captures the assassination, its agent (Tess), and its immediate consequence (fleet dissolution/succession crisis). Legitimate discrete beat, not a duplicate.

Type event.death is accurate. Year 135 AC correct (during the secret siege period). Identity statement accurately identifies Mervyn Flowers as the stabber (line 79: 'plunged it into Waters's belly'), Marston's title and role, the circumstances (arrest attempt during conspiracy exposure), and his fatal wound. Legitimate discrete event distinct from broader conspiracy narrative.

Type event.dismissal is accurate. Year 136 AC and 16th nameday are correct. Identity statement captures the specific moment and its scope: entry to council chamber, declaration of regency end, dismissal of three lot-chosen regents (Stackspear, Merryweather, Grandison), and dismissal of Hand Torrhen Manderly. This is a legitimate discrete beat—the momentary scene of dismissal/end-of-regency—distinct from the broader regency-of-aegon-iii period node. Edges are specific to the dismissal event and will not collide with regency-period edges. Proposal notes explicitly requested verification of this distinction and it holds.
